contentdetails.title: Power Content Details
contentdetails.description: Detailed information about the selected content item - properties, references, versions.

activeeditors.title: Active Editors
activeeditors.description: See who else is editing this content and collaborate with your team.

contenttypeaudit.title: Content Type Audit
contenttypeaudit.description: Overview of all content types, their usage, properties, and inheritance.

personalizationaudit.title: Personalization Audit
personalizationaudit.description: Find and audit visitor group usage across content, access rights, and properties.

audiencemanager.title: Audience Manager
audiencemanager.description: Manage and review visitor groups and audience segments.

contenttyperecommendations.title: Content Type Recommendations
contenttyperecommendations.description: Get recommendations for content type improvements and consolidation.

bulkpropertyeditor.title: Bulk Property Editor
bulkpropertyeditor.description: Edit properties across multiple content items at once.

scheduledjobsgantt.title: Scheduled Jobs Gantt
scheduledjobsgantt.description: Visualize scheduled job execution as a Gantt chart.

activitytimeline.title: Activity Timeline
activitytimeline.description: View a timeline of content changes and editorial activity.

contentimporter.title: Content Importer
contentimporter.description: Import content from CSV, JSON, or Excel files.

cmsdoctor.title: CMS Doctor
cmsdoctor.description: Health checks and diagnostics for your CMS installation.

contentaudit.title: Content Audit
contentaudit.description: Audit content for quality, completeness, and best practices.

linkchecker.title: Link Checker
linkchecker.description: Find and report broken links across your site.

activeeditors.title: Active Editors
activeeditors.description: See who is currently editing content and collaborate in real time.

managechildren.title: Manage Children
managechildren.description: Bulk manage child pages: sort, publish, unpublish, move, and delete.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Dashboard overview of all Editor Powertools features.

about.title: About Editor Powertools
about.description: About this plugin and the team behind it.
about.whatis_body: Editor Powertools is a free, open-source collection of power tools for Optimizely CMS 12 editors and administrators. It adds productivity tools, audit reports, and real-time collaboration features that are missing from the standard CMS — all accessible directly from the CMS navigation menu.
about.madeby_body: umage.ai is an agentic development company that connects people and software with AI. We help organizations modernize, build, and scale software that creates real business value — combining decades of consulting experience with the conviction that AI fundamentally transforms how software is built.

languageaudit.title: Language Audit
languageaudit.description: Analyze language coverage, find missing translations, and manage translation priorities.

linkaudit.title: Link Audit
linkaudit.description: Find and fix broken internal and external links across your content.

visitorgrouptester.title: Visitor Group Tester
visitorgrouptester.description: Floating toolbar to simulate visitor group membership and inspect editable content elements.

contentstatistics.title: Content Statistics
contentstatistics.description: Visual dashboard with charts showing content distribution, creation trends, staleness, and editor activity.

securityaudit.title: Security Audit
securityaudit.description: Analyze content permissions, find overly permissive access rights, and explore who can access what.

contentanalysis.description: Unified job that traverses all content once and runs all registered analyzers (content type stats, personalization, link checking, etc.).
contentanalysis.completed: Completed. Analyzed {0} content items with {1} analyzers ({2}).

cmsdoctor.runjobfirst: Run the '[EditorPowertools] Content Analysis' scheduled job first.

memorycheck.name: Memory Usage
memorycheck.description: Reports current memory usage of the application.
memorycheck.biton64: Running as 32-bit on 64-bit OS. Memory: {0} MB.
memorycheck.details: Working set: {0} MB, GC heap: {1} MB, 64-bit process: {2}, 64-bit OS: {3}

versioninfocheck.name: CMS Version
versioninfocheck.description: Reports the current Optimizely CMS version.

contenttypecheck.name: Content Type Count
contenttypecheck.description: Checks if the number of content types is within reasonable limits.
contenttypecheck.toomanypagetypes: Too many page types ({0}). Editors may struggle to find the right one.
contenttypecheck.toomanyblocktypes: Too many block types ({0}). Consider consolidating.
contenttypecheck.toofewpagetypes: Only {0} page type(s). Content may lack structure.
contenttypecheck.details: Page types: {0}, Block types: {1}, Media types: {2}, Total: {3}

orphanedpropertycheck.name: Orphaned Properties
orphanedpropertycheck.description: Finds properties defined in the database but not in code.
orphanedpropertycheck.found: {0} orphaned properties found in the database that are not defined in code.

scheduledjobscheck.name: Scheduled Jobs Health
scheduledjobscheck.description: Checks for failed or long-running scheduled jobs.

draftcontentcheck.name: Stale Drafts
draftcontentcheck.description: Checks for draft content that has never been published or has old unpublished changes.
draftcontentcheck.warning: {0} stale content items found. Consider reviewing and cleaning up.

brokenlinkscheck.name: Broken Links
brokenlinkscheck.description: Reports broken links found by the Link Checker tool.
brokenlinkscheck.nodata: No link data available. Run the Content Analysis scheduled job first.
brokenlinkscheck.nodatadetails: The Link Checker analyzer populates this data during the scheduled job.
brokenlinkscheck.fault: {0} broken links found across {1} total links.
brokenlinkscheck.notavailable: Link checker data not available yet.

missingalttextcheck.name: Missing Alt Text
missingalttextcheck.description: Checks for images missing alt text (accessibility and SEO issue).
missingalttextcheck.details: {0} of {1} images ({2}%) have no alt text. Last analyzed: {3}

unusedcontentcheck.name: Unused Content
unusedcontentcheck.description: Checks for blocks and media not referenced by anything (potential orphans).
unusedcontentcheck.ok: All blocks and media are referenced.
unusedcontentcheck.details: {0} of {1} blocks/media ({2}%) are unreferenced. Last analyzed: {3}

everyonepublishrightscheck.name: Everyone Publish Rights
everyonepublishrightscheck.description: Reports content where the "Everyone" role has Publish or higher access.
everyonepublishrightscheck.nodata: No security audit data available. Run the Content Analysis scheduled job first.
everyonepublishrightscheck.nodatadetails: The Security Audit analyzer populates this data during the scheduled job.
everyonepublishrightscheck.ok: No content grants Publish to the "Everyone" role.
everyonepublishrightscheck.warning: {0} content item(s) grant Publish or higher to the "Everyone" role.
everyonepublishrightscheck.notavailable: Security audit data not available yet.

unrestrictedcontentcheck.name: Unrestricted Content
unrestrictedcontentcheck.description: Reports pages with no access restrictions set.
unrestrictedcontentcheck.nodata: No security audit data available. Run the Content Analysis scheduled job first.
unrestrictedcontentcheck.nodatadetails: The Security Audit analyzer populates this data during the scheduled job.
unrestrictedcontentcheck.badpractice: {0} page(s) have no access restrictions (wide open).
unrestrictedcontentcheck.notavailable: Security audit data not available yet.

inconsistentinheritancecheck.name: Inconsistent Permission Inheritance
inconsistentinheritancecheck.description: Reports content that is more permissive than its parent, which may indicate accidental ACL changes.
inconsistentinheritancecheck.nodata: No security audit data available. Run the Content Analysis scheduled job first.
inconsistentinheritancecheck.nodatadetails: The Security Audit analyzer populates this data during the scheduled job.
inconsistentinheritancecheck.warning: {0} content item(s) are more permissive than their parent.
inconsistentinheritancecheck.notavailable: Security audit data not available yet.

errors.nomapping: No mapping configured. Run dry-run first.
errors.processingerror: An error occurred while processing the request.

contentaudit.empty_nofilter: No content matches the current filters.

languageaudit.btn_started: Job started, please refresh in a few minutes.
languageaudit.empty_all_translated: All content has been translated to {0}
languageaudit.empty_nodata: No language data available. Run the aggregation job to collect statistics.
languageaudit.banner_runjob: Run the [EditorPowertools] Content Analysis scheduled job to populate data.

cmsdoctor.header_desc: Health checks for your Optimizely CMS. Extensible by third-party packages.

contenttypeaudit.alert_running: Aggregation job is currently running. Content counts will be updated when it completes.
contenttypeaudit.alert_notrun: Content statistics have not been collected yet. The "Content" column will show data after the aggregation job has been run.
contenttypeaudit.alert_old: Statistics were last updated {0}. Consider running the aggregation job for fresh data.
contenttypeaudit.alert_started: Aggregation job has been started. Content counts will be updated when it completes.
contenttypeaudit.title_viewcontent: Click to view content of this type

linkchecker.alert_running: Link checker job is currently running. Results will be updated when it completes.
linkchecker.alert_notrun: Link checker has not been run yet. Run the scheduled job to scan content for links.

securityaudit.banner_runjob: Run the [EditorPowertools] Content Analysis scheduled job to analyze content permissions.
securityaudit.banner_started: Aggregation job has been started. Data will update when it completes.
securityaudit.alert_old: Security data was last analyzed {0}. Consider running the aggregation job for fresh data.
securityaudit.issue_everyonepublish: "Everyone" role has Publish or higher access
securityaudit.issue_everyoneedit: "Everyone" role has Edit or higher access
securityaudit.issue_morepermissive: This node is more permissive than its parent
securityaudit.issue_norestrictions: No access restrictions set on this page
securityaudit.empty_nodatarun: No data available. Run the scheduled job using the button above to populate security audit data.

bulkeditor.empty_selecttype: Select a content type above to start editing.
bulkeditor.empty_selecttype2: Select a content type above to start editing.
bulkeditor.empty_noitems: No content items found matching your criteria.

contentstatistics.banner_runjob: Run the [EditorPowertools] Content Analysis scheduled job to populate data.
contentstatistics.error_load: Failed to load statistics from API.
contentstatistics.empty_notype: No type statistics available. Run the scheduled job first.
contentstatistics.btn_jobstarted: Job started, please refresh in a few minutes.

activitytimeline.dlg_noprevious: No previous version available for comparison.
activitytimeline.dlg_nochanges: No property changes detected between these versions.
activitytimeline.empty_noactivities: No activities found matching the current filters.

personalizationaudit.alert_running: Personalization analysis job is currently running. Results will be updated when it completes.
personalizationaudit.alert_notrun: Personalization usage has not been analyzed yet. Run the analysis job to scan content for audience usage.
personalizationaudit.alert_old: Analysis was last run {0}. Consider running the job again for fresh data.
personalizationaudit.empty_nousages: No personalization usage data available. Run the analysis job first.
personalizationaudit.alert_jobstarted: Personalization analysis job has been started. Results will be updated when it completes.

audiencemanager.title_notused: Not used in any personalized content
audiencemanager.title_runjobfirst: Run the Personalization Analysis job first

contentimporter.lbl_dropfile: Drop a CSV, JSON, or Excel file here, or click to browse

managechildren.lbl_selectparent: Select a parent page to manage its children
managechildren.confirm_delete: Delete {0} selected item(s)? This cannot be undone.

recommendations.banner_info: When editors create new content, Optimizely can suggest which content types to use. Define rules below to control these suggestions based on where content is being created.
recommendations.empty_norules: No recommendation rules defined yet. Click "Add Rule" to create one.
recommendations.err_selecttype: Please select at least one content type to suggest.

activeeditors.error_nosignalr: SignalR client not available. Check browser console for errors.
activeeditors.dlg_desc: This will send a CMS notification that {0} will see in their notification bell.
activeeditors.empty_selectcontent: Select content to see active editors.

contentdetails.empty_uses: This content does not reference other content.

help.contenttypeaudit:
The Content Type Audit gives you a complete picture of every content type registered in your solution — how many times each is used, how many properties it defines versus inherits, and where it sits in the inheritance hierarchy. Run the built-in job first to populate usage counts, then switch between the table view for sorting and filtering and the tree view for visualising inheritance.
Use this tool to identify unused or rarely-used types that are candidates for removal, spot content types that have grown bloated with too many properties, and understand how your content model has evolved over time. The export function produces a CSV covering all types and their properties.
help.personalizationaudit:
The Personalization Audit maps every place in your content tree where visitor groups are referenced — access restrictions, content area personalizations, and XHTML-string inline personalizations. Run the background job to perform the full scan, then filter results by visitor group, content type, or usage type.
Use this tool before removing or renaming a visitor group to verify it is no longer in use, to audit how broadly personalization has been applied across the site, or to hand off a report to a stakeholder showing personalization coverage. The export gives a flat CSV of every usage found.
help.audiencemanager:
Audience Manager is a consolidated view of every visitor group defined in the CMS. For each group you can see its criteria, the logical operator (All/Any), usage statistics (if the statistics job has run), and a direct count of content items that reference it.
Use it alongside the Personalization Audit to maintain a clean audience library. Groups with zero usage and no recent activity are good candidates for removal. You can open any group directly in the CMS visitor group editor from the usage details dialog.
help.contenttyperecommendations:
Content Type Recommendations maintains a set of rules that suggest which content types should be available when an editor creates content under a specific parent. Rules are stored in the database and evaluated at content creation time, filtering the type picker to show only relevant options.
Create rules by specifying a parent content type (optionally scoped to a specific content location) and a list of allowed child types. The tool is useful for enforcing editorial governance — for example, ensuring that only specific block types appear inside a carousel container, or that only press releases can be created under the News folder.
help.bulkpropertyeditor:
The Bulk Property Editor lets you update a single property across many content items in one operation. Select a content type from the dropdown, the tool loads all published instances with that type and presents them in a paginated table. Click a cell to edit it inline, make changes across multiple rows, then save or publish all pending changes at once.
Use it to mass-correct data quality issues — for example, clearing a deprecated field, applying a category tag to all items of a type, or setting a default value that was missing when the content was created. You can filter by content location to scope the operation to a specific subtree.
help.scheduledjobsgantt:
The Scheduled Jobs Gantt chart renders the execution history of every scheduled job as a horizontal timeline. Each bar represents one run — its width shows duration, its colour shows the outcome (success, failure, or running). Bars are grouped by job name in rows, so you can immediately see which jobs ran at the same time and how long each took.
Use it to diagnose contention between jobs, investigate jobs that are taking longer than expected, or confirm that a failing job is recurring. Navigate forward and backward in time using the arrow buttons, and zoom in or out to change the visible time window. Hover a bar to see start time, end time, duration, and status.
help.activitytimeline:
The Activity Timeline is a chronological feed of every recorded content action across the site — publications, draft saves, moves, deletions, and workflow transitions. Each entry shows the editor responsible, a timestamp, and a direct link to the affected content item in edit mode.
Filter by date range, editor username, content type, or action type to narrow the view to exactly what you are looking for. The timeline is paginated and loads more entries as you scroll. Use it for editorial audits, compliance reviews, post-incident investigations, or simply to understand what has been happening on the site over a given period.
help.contentimporter:
The Content Importer creates content items in bulk from a structured file. Upload a CSV, JSON, or Excel file, then map each column in the file to a property on the target content type. Choose the parent location in the content tree where items will be created, optionally set a master language, and decide whether imported items should be published immediately.
Run a dry-run first to validate the mapping and preview which items would be created and what their property values would be. The dry-run highlights rows with validation errors before any data is written. Then run the full import — progress is shown row by row and any failures are listed with their error reason so you can fix the source file and re-import.
help.cmsdoctor:
CMS Doctor runs a suite of diagnostic checks against your Optimizely installation and presents each result with a status badge (OK, Warning, or Fault) and an explanation of what was found. Checks cover areas including server memory, orphaned content type properties, broken internal links, images missing alt text, content accessible without authentication, and scheduled job health.
Run all checks at once with the Run All button, or run individual checks on demand. Checks that find issues include remediation guidance, and some offer a one-click fix action. The tool is extensible — third-party NuGet packages can register additional checks by implementing the check interface and registering via dependency injection.
help.contentaudit:
The Content Audit provides a full inventory of every content item in the CMS as a searchable, filterable, sortable table. Columns include content type, status, language, creation and modification dates, the editor who last changed the item, incoming reference count, and version count. Use the column picker to show or hide columns, and add filters to narrow results by any combination of fields.
Export the visible result set to CSV, Excel, or JSON for use in reports or further analysis. Combine filters to answer questions like "which pages have never been published", "which blocks are referenced by nothing", or "which content items were modified last week". The tool supports server-side pagination and handles large content trees without loading everything into memory at once.
help.linkchecker:
The Link Checker scans all published content for broken links — both internal links to other content items and external URLs. It runs as a background job that crawls every content item, extracts all link property values and XHTML string links, then checks each one. Internal links are validated against the content repository; external links are checked with an HTTP request.
After the job completes, the results table shows every broken URL alongside the content items that reference it, with a direct link to each item in the CMS edit view. Re-run the job at any time to refresh results. Use the filters to focus on just internal or just external links, or to filter by HTTP status code. Regular runs as part of a quality routine help catch regressions before they are noticed by site visitors.
help.activeeditors:
Active Editors shows in real time who is currently logged in as an editor and what content they have open. Presence data is broadcast via SignalR and updates automatically as editors navigate, open content, and log out. Each editor entry shows their username, the content item they are currently viewing or editing, and how long they have been active.
The built-in chat lets you send a message to all active editors simultaneously — useful for broadcast announcements like "deploying in 5 minutes, please save your work". The tool is also available as a CMS assets panel widget so editors can see presence information without leaving the content editing view.
help.managechildren:
Manage Children lets you work with all the child pages of a selected parent in a single bulk-operation view. After picking a parent from the content tree, the tool lists every direct child with its name, publish status, content type, and last-modified date. Select one or more items using the checkboxes to unlock the bulk action toolbar.
Available bulk actions include publish, unpublish, move to another parent (with a content tree picker), send to trash, and permanent delete. You can also reorder children by dragging rows into a new sequence and saving the order. Use this tool to reorganise large sections of the site, publish a batch of pages at once after a content freeze, or clean up obsolete content structures without opening each item individually.
help.securityaudit:
The Security Audit analyses access rights across your content tree and surfaces potential security issues. Run the background job to collect permission data, then navigate the results using three tabs: the Tree tab shows a hierarchical view of content with permission indicators and highlights nodes where ACLs differ from the parent; the Roles tab shows a matrix of role-to-content permissions; and the Issues tab lists specific problems found.
Issues detected include content where the Everyone role has publish or edit rights, content items that are less restricted than their parent (inheritance broken in a permissive direction), and content with no access restrictions at all. Use the tool before go-live, after restructuring the content tree, or as part of a regular security review cycle.
help.contentstatistics:
Content Statistics presents charts and metrics summarising editorial activity across your site. Charts include content distribution by type (pages vs blocks vs media), content creation and modification volume over time (daily or weekly buckets), editor activity rankings (who has published the most, who has been most active recently), and content staleness (items not updated in more than 30, 90, or 180 days).
Run the background job to compute the statistics — for large sites this may take several minutes. After the job completes, the charts render from the pre-computed data and load quickly. Use the tool to track editorial momentum, identify inactive areas of the site that may need attention, report on content production to stakeholders, or benchmark activity before and after an editorial process change.
help.languageaudit:
The Language Audit reports on translation coverage across every language enabled on your site. The Overview tab shows total content counts per language and a coverage percentage. The Missing tab lists every content item that exists in the master language but has no version at all in one or more other languages. The Stale tab shows items where the master language version has been updated significantly more recently than a translation, suggesting the translation may be out of date.
Configure the staleness threshold to define how many days of lag counts as stale. Use the Translation Queue tab to generate an exportable list of items needing translation, sorted by priority. Filter by language pair, content type, subtree, or staleness threshold to focus on the most important gaps. Run the background job regularly to keep the data current.
help.contentdetails:
Power Content Details is a CMS assets panel widget that shows in-depth information about whatever content item you currently have selected in the CMS. The Info tab displays key metadata: content ID, GUID, type, status, language, parent, creation date, last modified date, and the editor who made the last change. The Properties tab lists every property value.
The Used By tab shows all content items that reference the current item — useful for understanding the impact of editing a shared block or media file. The Uses tab shows all content items that the current item references. The Versions tab lists every saved version with its status and the editor who created it, and lets you compare two versions side by side. The widget updates automatically as you navigate between content items in the CMS.
